text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint little village surrounded by vast fields of sunflowers, lived a girl named Sophie. She loved to wander off into the vast meadows, where the wind would whistle through the leaves of fields of wildflowers. One afternoon, as the sun began it's daily descent toward the horizon, casting a warm gold glow upon everything it touched, Sophie found herself stumbling upon an unusual sight.

There, in the middle of nowhere, was what appeared to be an old man, sitting cross-legged under the shade of an ancient oak tree. He wore worn-out clothes, a pair of spectacles perched on the end of his nose, and his hair was a messy mop of grey. The old man seemed entirely unbothered by his solitary place in the world.

"Good evening," Sophie said, approaching cautiously. "I wasn't expecting to find anyone here."
